title: quercus repanda
intro: Quercus repanda, also known as the Himalayan oak, is a species of oak tree native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Quercus repanda include reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and acting as an anti-inflammatory agent to alleviate pain and swelling. Quercus repanda can be prepared into herbal teas, decoctions, or infusions, and is also used in the form of powder or capsules as a dietary supplement.
